Catalog Description

Special Topics in... Credit 0 to 4. Selected topics in an identified area of computer

science. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: Approval of instructor.

Course Prerequisites

Graduate classification  Approval of instructor.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Evaluate properties of real and synthetic networks using several C++ and

Python software packages

Develop and evaluate graph machine learning models, including graph neural

networks and recommendation systems.

Create knowledge graphs from unstructured data and perform graph

embedding for link predictions in knowledge graphs.

Read research papers on graph machine learning and compare various state-

of-the-art methods

Develop a comprehensive project related to graph machine learning.

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

1. Projects (proposal, presentation, and report):  30%

2. Midterm exam: 30%

3. Homework:   30%

4. Paper Reading and Presentation: 10%

5. The grading scale will be:

A.  = 90-100

B.  = 80-89

C.  = 70-79

D.  = 60-69 F = <60

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late submissions will incur a 20% penalty for each day past the deadline. Assignments

submitted more than 4 days late will receive no credit.

Course Schedule

Course Schedule

Week

Topics

1

Overview of graph analysis, definitions, characterizing graphs; Analyzing

characterizing

practical graphs using Python and NetworkX

2

Introduction to random graph models; Compare random graphs with real

graphs

Week

Topics

3

Graph embedding models, such as deepwalk and node2vec; Implement

graph embedding

using random walks

4

Introduction to graph neural networks (GNNs)

5

Advanced graph neural network model

6

Interpretable graph neural networks; Scalable training methods for GNNs

7

Introduction to recommendation systems

Midterm Exam

8

Introduction to knowledge graphs; Knowledge graph embedding

9

Knowledge graph representation learning

10

Introduction to graph databases

11

Integrating knowledge graphs with large language models

12

GraphRAG-based LLM

13

Scientific applications with graph ML and knowledge graphs

14

Research paper reading

15

Project final presentation
